data.content:
Here's my analysis of the build infrastructure across all 5 project files:

---

## Build Infrastructure Review

### Issues Found

**1. No `Directory.Build.props` — all common properties are copy-pasted**

Every project repeats these 7 properties identically:
```xml
<TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
<LangVersion>latest</LangVersion>
<Nullable>enable</Nullable>
<ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
<TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
<EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
<Company>Contoso</Company>
<Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
```

**2. Package version drift (no Central Package Management)**

| Package | Api/Core | Services |
|---------|----------|----------|
| `Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions` | 8.0.0 | **8.0.1** |

| Test Package | Core.Tests | Api.Tests |
|--------------|-----------|-----------|
| `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` | 17.10.0 | **17.9.0** |
| `xunit` | 2.8.1 | **2.7.1** |
| `xunit.runner.visualstudio` | 2.8.1 | **2.5.6** |

**3. Duplicated `PrintBuildInfo` target** — identical in 3 source projects.

**4. Duplicated `DocumentationFile` property** — identical in 3 source projects.

**5. Test infrastructure repeated** — both test projects list the same 4 test packages.

---

### Recommended Solution

#### A) Add `Directory.Build.props` at the repo root

```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <LangVersion>latest</LangVersion>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
    <EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
    <Company>Contoso</Company>
    <Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- Source projects generate XML docs -->
  <PropertyGroup Condition="!$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <DocumentationFile>$(OutputPath)$(AssemblyName).xml</DocumentationFile>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- Test projects are not packable -->
  <PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

#### B) Add `Directory.Build.targets` at the repo root

```xml
<Project>
  <Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="Build"
          Condition="!$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <Message Importance="high" Text="Building $(AssemblyName) [$(Configuration)]" />
  </Target>
</Project>
```

#### C) Enable Central Package Management with `Directory.Packages.props`

```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <!-- Shared -->
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" Version="8.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="8.0.5" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Newtonsoft.Json" Version="13.0.3" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Swashbuckle.AspNetCore" Version="6.5.0" />
    <!-- Test -->
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.10.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.8.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="NSubstitute" Version="5.1.0" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

Then each `.csproj` drops the `Version=` attribute from `<PackageReference>` items.

#### D) Resulting slimmed-down project files

After applying A–C, `ContosoApp.Core.csproj` shrinks from 27 lines to:

```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" />
    <PackageReference Include="System.Text.Json" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

---

### Summary of benefits

| Before | After |
|--------|-------|
| 7 common properties × 5 projects = 35 lines | 1 place to maintain |
| 9 distinct package versions scattered across 5 files | 1 `Directory.Packages.props` governs all versions |
| Version drift between test projects | Impossible by design |
| Custom target pasted 3× | Defined once in `.targets` |
| Adding a new project requires remembering all conventions | New projects inherit everything automatically |

Would you like me to implement these changes?
data.reasoningText:
Now I have a clear picture of the build infrastructure. Let me analyze the issues:

1. **Massive duplication across all 5 project files:**
   - `TargetFramework`, `LangVersion`, `Nullable`, `ImplicitUsings`, `TreatWarningsAsErrors`, `EnforceCodeStyleInBuild`, `Company`, `Authors` are repeated in every single project.
   
2. **No `Directory.Build.props` or `Directory.Build.targets`** — This is the primary MSBuild mechanism for centralizing common properties.

3. **Package version inconsistencies:**
   - `Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions` varies between `8.0.0` and `8.0.1` across projects, and test packages like `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`, `xunit`, and `xunit.runner.visualstudio` have different versions scattered throughout the test projects.

4. **No Central Package Management (Directory.Packages.props)** — Modern .NET solutions should use this to manage all package versions in one place.

5. **Duplicated custom target** — The `PrintBuildInfo` target is copy-pasted across all three source projects.

6. **Duplicated `DocumentationFile` property** — This setting is repeated unnecessarily across all three source projects.

7. **Test project duplication** — Both test projects repeat the same test infrastructure packages and common properties like `IsPackable`, which should be consolidated.

I'm ready to provide a comprehensive recommendation to address these issues.
